default.incidentDescription: A targeted phishing email was sent to several employees of the engineering team. The message impersonated the IT helpdesk and asked recipients to log into a fake portal to renew their VPN credentials. Two employees reported the message before any credentials were submitted; the link domain has been blocked at the firewall.
default.suggestedActions:
- Rotate VPN credentials for the affected team
- Add the impersonating domain to the email gateway block list
- Send a phishing-awareness reminder to all employees
- Open a follow-up ticket with the security awareness training vendor

ddos-critical.description: Sustained DDoS against the public API.
ddos-critical.incidentDescription: Beginning at 03:14 UTC, the public REST API began receiving sustained traffic exceeding 12x normal peak. Layer-7 requests were focused on the `/v1/search` endpoint with high cardinality payloads. WAF rules were applied at 03:22 UTC; traffic returned to baseline by 04:05 UTC. Two customer reports of 5xx errors during the window.
ddos-critical.suggestedActions:
- Confirm WAF rules are committed and reviewed by team lead
- Reach out to the two affected customers with a status note
- Schedule a post-mortem within 48 hours
- Investigate whether to enable cache headers on `/v1/search`

data-breach-low.incidentDescription: An internal Google Doc was set to `anyone-with-the-link can view` for approximately 90 minutes before being corrected by the document owner. The document contained an internal roadmap with no customer or financial data. Access logs were reviewed; no external views were recorded.
data-breach-low.suggestedActions:
- Run a sharing-audit script across the team drive
- Remind document owners about default sharing settings
- File a low-severity ticket and close after the audit is complete
